metadata.name: FERS Employee Contribution Rate History
metadata.description: Historical FERS employee contribution rates by hire date cohort. Rates differ based on when an employee entered federal service.
sources: Congressional Research Service: Federal Employees' Retirement System (RL30387)
notes: FERS employees contribute a percentage of basic pay toward their defined benefit pension.
Three cohorts exist based on hire date, created by FERS-RAE (2013) and FERS-FRAE (2014).
FERS: Hired before 1/1/2013 — 0.8% of basic pay (original rate since 1987).
FERS-FRAE: Hired 1/1/2014 or later — 4.4% of basic pay.
Government contributes the balance needed to fund the pension (currently ~13-15% of pay).
Congressional/elected official FERS rates are higher: 1.3%, 3.6%, and 4.9% respectively.
Law enforcement/firefighter FERS rates: 1.3%, 3.6%, and 4.9% respectively.

cohorts.law: Federal Employees' Retirement System Act of 1986
Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act of 2012 (P.L. 112-96)

csrs_reference.notes: CSRS is closed to new entrants. Rate has been 7% since inception.

fers_benefit_formula.high_3_note: Highest 3 consecutive years of basic pay (usually last 3 years before retirement)

timeline.rate_change: No rate changes. Three cohorts remain active.
